summary: Frontend-only validation of the new AdMob Native ad integration on Digital Shield (Expo web preview). Verified notification shade injection of FakeSponsorCard (non-Android fallback for AdMob NativeAd), close mechanisms (close button + backdrop), notification ordering, regressions on /skins, /settings, and lock→launcher, plus adConfig.ts export shape with live Android ad unit IDs.
action_items: Non-blocking: React Native web warnings appear in console for deprecated style props: 'textShadow*' (use 'textShadow'), 'shadow*' (use 'boxShadow'), and 'props.pointerEvents' (use 'style.pointerEvents'). These are RN Web deprecation warnings, not blockers, but worth cleaning up.

Reminder from adConfig.ts comment: Android APP ID (ca-app-pub-5626622356708517~XXXXXXXXXX) still needs to be filled into app.json plugins → react-native-google-mobile-ads → androidAppId before publishing, otherwise the real Android build will crash on startup.
critical_code_review_comments: adConfig.ts export shape is correct: adUnitIDs.banner, adUnitIDs.native, adUnitIDs.interstitial, adUnitIDs.rewarded all present, non-empty, and prefixed with 'ca-app-pub-' across DEV, PROD Android, and PROD non-Android branches.

NotificationShade.tsx correctly gates real NativeAdCard behind isAdsAvailable and falls back to FakeSponsorCard on iOS/Expo Go/web. Insertion index (idx === 1, i.e. after 2nd notification) matches the requirement.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Digital Shield is Expo web preview. Real AdMob NativeAd/BannerAd/RewardedAd only initialize on Android native builds; web preview always shows FakeSponsorCard/FakeAdBanner/simulated rewarded modal. Do not attempt to load real ads in preview.
rca_of_issue: N/A — all listed test cases pass.

detailed_results.shade_open: PASS — testID='open-shade-btn' opens the shade; testID='notification-shade' becomes visible with pointerEvents=auto.
detailed_results.fake_sponsor_visible: PASS — testID='fake-native-sponsor-card' visible inside open shade with all required content: headline 'Level up your focus with premium widgets', 'AD' badge, body containing 'Sponsored placement', and 'LEARN MORE' CTA.
detailed_results.notification_order: PASS — visible order confirmed in screenshot: Focus Streak (notif), Memory Stable (notif), FakeSponsorCard (Level up… AD), Insight Available (notif), Security Updated (notif). Order = notif, notif, fake, notif, notif.
detailed_results.close_button: PASS — testID='notif-close-btn' smoothly closes shade (pointerEvents becomes 'none' after animation).
detailed_results.backdrop_close: PASS — testID='notif-shade-backdrop' also closes shade (pointerEvents becomes 'none' after animation).
detailed_results.skins_regression: PASS — 7 unique skins rendered with per-skin testIDs (unlock-*/active-*/apply-*): amber, cyber-blue, dark-red, glass-white, gold, matrix-green, neon-purple.
detailed_results.settings_regression: PASS — All segmented controls present: seg-haptic (off/light/medium/strong), seg-particle (low/medium/high), seg-anim (slow/normal/fast). Vibration + Sounds toggles and Restore purchases visible.
detailed_results.lock_to_launcher: PASS — Lock screen shows testID='lock-unlock-btn'; tapping it navigates to /launcher and testID='open-shade-btn' becomes present.
detailed_results.adConfig_shape: PASS — In all three resolution branches (DEV, PROD-Android, PROD-non-Android) the exported adUnitIDs object has non-empty string values for banner/native/interstitial/rewarded, all starting with 'ca-app-pub-'. Production Android banner=ca-app-pub-5626622356708517/4259340010, native=ca-app-pub-5626622356708517/8099201038 match the user-provided live ad unit IDs; interstitial/rewarded correctly fall back to Google test IDs pending unit creation.
